annotations.label: Möbius strip (definition)

anchor.snippet: is a surface that can be formed by attaching the ends of a strip of paper together with a half-twist

annotations.note: Grep for "Möbius" in Mathlib returns only the number-theoretic Möbius function; the Möbius strip surface is not defined.

annotations.label: Non-orientability

anchor.snippet: if an asymmetric two-dimensional object slides one time around the strip, it returns to its starting position as its mirror image

annotations.note: Orientability of manifolds appears only in comments in ChartedSpace.lean, not as a formal definition.

annotations.label: Simplest non-orientable surface

anchor.snippet: any other surface is non-orientable if and only if it has a Möbius strip as a subset

annotations.note: Neither surface orientability nor the Möbius strip is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: One-sidedness when embedded in Euclidean space

anchor.snippet: the Möbius strip has only one side

annotations.note: No Möbius strip or notion of surface sidedness exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Two-sided embeddings in other spaces

anchor.snippet: there exist other topological spaces in which the Möbius strip can be embedded so that it has two sides

annotations.note: No Möbius strip definition exists, so its embeddings are not formalized.

annotations.label: Only countably many disjoint embeddings

anchor.snippet: only a countable number of Möbius strips can be simultaneously embedded

annotations.note: No Möbius strip in Mathlib, so this embedding cardinality result is absent.

annotations.label: Single boundary curve

anchor.snippet: includes all boundary points of the Möbius strip in a single continuous curve

annotations.note: No Möbius strip definition or its boundary curve is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Boundary length is twice the centerline

anchor.snippet: it has twice the length of the centerline of the strip

annotations.note: No Möbius strip metric/boundary-length facts exist in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Chirality of the Möbius strip

anchor.snippet: it is a chiral object with right- or left-handedness

annotations.note: No Möbius strip or chirality-of-embedding notion is formalized.

annotations.label: Equivalence of embeddings (knot and twists)

anchor.snippet: two Möbius strips are equivalently embedded in three-dimensional space when their centerlines determine the same knot

annotations.note: Mathlib has no knot theory or Möbius strip embedding equivalence.

annotations.label: Even twists yield the annulus

anchor.snippet: one obtains a different topological surface, called the annulus

annotations.note: Neither the annulus surface nor this twist construction is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Deformation retraction onto centerline

anchor.snippet: This transformation is an example of a deformation retraction

annotations.note: Grep for deformation retraction returns no results; Mathlib has HomotopyEquiv but not this specific retraction.

annotations.label: Fundamental group is infinite cyclic

anchor.snippet: its fundamental group is the same as the fundamental group of a circle, an infinite cyclic group

annotations.note: The FundamentalGroup is defined generally, but π₁ of the circle (or Möbius strip) being ℤ is not proven in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cutting along the centerline

anchor.snippet: Cutting a Möbius strip along the centerline with a pair of scissors yields one long strip with four half-twists in it

annotations.note: No Möbius strip or cutting operation exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cutting a third of the way across

anchor.snippet: If, instead, a Möbius strip is cut lengthwise, a third of the way across its width, it produces two linked strips

annotations.note: No Möbius strip or cutting operation exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Paradromic rings

anchor.snippet: are sometimes called paradromic rings

annotations.note: Paradromic rings are not defined anywhere in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Six mutually adjacent regions / six colors

anchor.snippet: The Möbius strip can be cut into six mutually adjacent regions, showing that maps on the surface of the Möbius strip can sometimes require six colors

annotations.note: Mathlib has no map-coloring theory on surfaces (no four/six-color results).

annotations.label: Ringel–Youngs theorem

anchor.snippet: This result is part of the Ringel–Youngs theorem

annotations.note: The Ringel–Youngs map-color theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tietze's graph

anchor.snippet: The edges and vertices of these six regions form Tietze's graph

annotations.note: Tietze's graph is not defined in Mathlib's SimpleGraph library.

annotations.label: Möbius ladders

anchor.snippet: are the Möbius ladders

annotations.note: Grep for Möbius ladder returns no results in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Three utilities problem on a Möbius strip

anchor.snippet: the three utilities problem can be solved on a transparent Möbius strip

annotations.note: Mathlib has no graph planarity theory and no Möbius strip embedding of K₃,₃.

annotations.label: Euler characteristic is zero

anchor.snippet: The Euler characteristic of the Möbius strip is zero

annotations.note: Mathlib's EulerCharacteristic is for chain complexes, not surfaces, and the Möbius strip is undefined.

annotations.label: Construction by sweeping a line segment

anchor.snippet: One way to embed the Möbius strip in three-dimensional Euclidean space is to sweep it out by a line segment rotating in a plane

annotations.note: This explicit swept-segment embedding is not defined in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: One way to embed the Möbius strip in three-dimensional Euclidean space is to sweep it out by a line segment rotating in a plane

annotations.label: Any odd number of half-twists

anchor.snippet: The same method can produce Möbius strips with any odd number of half-twists, by rotating the segment more quickly in its plane

annotations.note: No Möbius strip construction or twist parameter exists in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: The same method can produce Möbius strips with any odd number of half-twists, by rotating the segment more quickly in its plane

annotations.label: Sliced torus remains connected

anchor.snippet: Because of the one-sidedness of this slice, the sliced torus remains connected

annotations.note: This slicing-of-torus statement involving Möbius geometry is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: Because of the one-sidedness of this slice, the sliced torus remains connected

annotations.label: Plücker's conoid

anchor.snippet: forms Plücker's conoid or cylindroid, an algebraic ruled surface in the form of a self-crossing Möbius strip

annotations.note: Plücker's conoid and ruled surfaces are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Shortest flat-folded equilateral-triangle strip

anchor.snippet: The shortest strip for which this is possible consists of three equilateral triangles, folded at the edges where two triangles meet

annotations.note: Flat-folded polyhedral Möbius strips are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Trihexaflexagon

anchor.snippet: For a strip of nine equilateral triangles, the result is a trihexaflexagon

annotations.note: Flexagons are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Five-triangle polyhedral Möbius strip

anchor.snippet: with only five triangular faces sharing five vertices

annotations.note: No polyhedral Möbius strip model exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Unique tight Möbius strip

anchor.snippet: This four-dimensional polyhedral Möbius strip is the only tight Möbius strip

annotations.note: Tight polyhedral surfaces and this uniqueness result are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Other polyhedral embeddings

anchor.snippet: Other polyhedral embeddings of Möbius strips include one with four convex quadrilaterals as faces

annotations.note: Polyhedral embeddings of the Möbius strip are not formalized.

annotations.label: Triangulations of the projective plane embed

anchor.snippet: Every abstract triangulation of the projective plane can be embedded into 3D as a polyhedral Möbius strip with a triangular boundary after removing one of its faces

annotations.note: Mathlib has no triangulation theory of the projective plane nor polyhedral Möbius embeddings.

annotations.label: Realizability condition for triangulations

anchor.snippet: it is necessary and sufficient that there be no two disjoint non-contractible 3-cycles in the triangulation

annotations.note: This combinatorial realizability criterion is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Smooth embedding aspect ratio bound

anchor.snippet: can be embedded smoothly into three-dimensional space whenever its aspect ratio is greater than

annotations.note: Aspect-ratio bounds for smooth Möbius strip embeddings are not formalized.

annotations.label: Developable surface model of paper

anchor.snippet: a smoothly embedded sheet of paper can be modeled as a developable surface

annotations.note: Grep finds no developable-surface definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lower limit on smooth aspect ratio

anchor.snippet: there is a lower limit to the aspect ratio of smooth rectangular Möbius strips

annotations.note: No aspect-ratio results for smooth Möbius strips exist in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: there is a lower limit to the aspect ratio of smooth rectangular Möbius strips

annotations.label: Self-intersecting strips above the bound

anchor.snippet: Self-intersecting smooth Möbius strips exist for any aspect ratio above this bound

annotations.note: This existence statement about self-intersecting Möbius strips is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: Self-intersecting smooth Möbius strips exist for any aspect ratio above this bound

annotations.label: Aspect-ratio bound without self-intersection

anchor.snippet: Without self-intersections, the aspect ratio must be at least

annotations.note: This embedded-aspect-ratio bound is not in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: Without self-intersections, the aspect ratio must be at least

annotations.label: Schwartz: no smooth embeddings below cutoff

anchor.snippet: In 2023, Richard Schwartz announced a proof that they do not exist

annotations.note: Schwartz's optimal Möbius aspect-ratio theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Nash–Kuiper theorem application

anchor.snippet: the Nash–Kuiper theorem implies that any two opposite edges of any rectangle can be glued to form an embedded Möbius strip

annotations.note: Grep for Nash–Kuiper returns no results; the theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Unbounded Möbius strip / tautological line bundle

anchor.snippet: is called the unbounded Möbius strip or the real tautological line bundle

annotations.note: Grep for tautological line bundle returns no results in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Smooth closed embedding in 4D

anchor.snippet: it can be embedded smoothly as a closed subset of four-dimensional Euclidean space

annotations.note: No Möbius strip embedding into 4D space is formalized.

annotations.label: Minimum-energy shape

anchor.snippet: The minimum-energy shape of a smooth Möbius strip glued from a rectangle does not have a known analytic description, but can be calculated numerically

annotations.note: Bending-energy minimization of Möbius strips is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Boundary is topologically a circle

anchor.snippet: of a Möbius strip is topologically equivalent to a circle

annotations.note: No Möbius strip definition exists, so its boundary's homeomorphism type is not formalized.

annotations.label: Klein bottle from two Möbius strips

anchor.snippet: A Klein bottle is the surface that results when two Möbius strips are glued together edge-to-edge

annotations.note: Grep for Klein bottle returns no results in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Circular slice of Lawson's Klein bottle

anchor.snippet: the curve along which it is sliced can be made circular, resulting in Möbius strips with circular edges

annotations.note: Lawson's Klein bottle and its slices are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lawson's Klein bottle

anchor.snippet: Lawson's Klein bottle is a self-crossing minimal surface in the unit hypersphere of 4-dimensional space

annotations.note: Minimal surfaces and Lawson's Klein bottle are not defined in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: Lawson's Klein bottle is a self-crossing minimal surface in the unit hypersphere of 4-dimensional space

annotations.label: Sudanese Möbius strip

anchor.snippet: after topologists Sue Goodman and Daniel Asimov

annotations.note: The Sudanese Möbius strip is not defined in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: after topologists Sue Goodman and Daniel Asimov

annotations.label: Symmetry group of the Sudanese strip

anchor.snippet: leaves it with an infinite group of symmetries isomorphic to the orthogonal group

annotations.note: Though Matrix.orthogonalGroup exists, the Sudanese strip and its symmetry group are not formalized.

anchors.snippet: leaves it with an infinite group of symmetries isomorphic to the orthogonal group

annotations.label: Cross-cap

anchor.snippet: Another form of the Möbius strip, called the cross-cap or crosscap

annotations.note: The cross-cap surface is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Open Möbius strip

anchor.snippet: The open Möbius strip is the relative interior of a standard Möbius strip, formed by omitting the points on its boundary edge

annotations.note: No Möbius strip (open or closed) is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Constant-curvature Riemannian metrics

anchor.snippet: It may be given a Riemannian geometry of constant positive, negative, or zero Gaussian curvature

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks Gaussian curvature of surfaces and the Möbius strip.

annotations.label: Unique flat complete metric

anchor.snippet: This is the unique metric on the Möbius strip, up to uniform scaling, that is both flat and complete

annotations.note: This uniqueness result for a flat complete metric on the Möbius strip is not formalized.

annotations.label: One of five complete flat manifolds

anchor.snippet: is one of only five two-dimensional complete flat manifolds

annotations.note: The classification of 2D complete flat manifolds is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Complete negative-curvature metric

anchor.snippet: The open Möbius strip also admits complete metrics of constant negative curvature

annotations.note: No constant-negative-curvature metrics on the Möbius strip are formalized.

annotations.label: One of 13 nonstandard surfaces

anchor.snippet: is one of only 13 nonstandard surfaces

annotations.note: This classification of nonstandard hyperbolic surfaces is not formalized.

annotations.label: Positive curvature cannot be complete

anchor.snippet: A Möbius strip of constant positive curvature cannot be complete

annotations.note: This completeness obstruction is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Homeomorphic to once-punctured projective plane

anchor.snippet: the open Möbius strip is homeomorphic to the once-punctured projective plane

annotations.note: Mathlib has Projectivization but no real projective plane surface nor Möbius strip homeomorphism.

annotations.label: Meeks Möbius strip

anchor.snippet: there is also a unique complete (boundaryless) minimal surface immersed in Euclidean space that has the topology of an open Möbius strip

annotations.note: Minimal surfaces and the Meeks Möbius strip are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Construction via the Björling problem

anchor.snippet: can be constructed using solutions to the Björling problem

annotations.note: The Björling problem for minimal surfaces is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Space of plane lines is the open Möbius strip

anchor.snippet: The resulting space of lines is topologically equivalent to the open Möbius strip

annotations.note: The topology of the space of lines in the plane is not identified with a Möbius strip in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Space of Euclidean lines as punctured projective plane

anchor.snippet: the space of Euclidean lines is a punctured projective plane, which is one of the forms of the open Möbius strip

annotations.note: This topological identification of the line space is not formalized.

annotations.label: Space of hyperbolic lines

anchor.snippet: The space of lines in the hyperbolic plane can be parameterized by unordered pairs of distinct points on a circle

annotations.note: The space of hyperbolic lines and its parameterization are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Open Möbius strip is homogeneous

anchor.snippet: the open Möbius strip is a homogeneous space

annotations.note: Grep finds no homogeneous-space definition, and the Möbius strip is undefined.

annotations.label: Solvmanifold that is not a nilmanifold

anchor.snippet: showing that not every solvmanifold is a nilmanifold

annotations.note: Grep for nilmanifold/solvmanifold returns no results in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Group model

anchor.snippet: A group model consists of a Lie group and a stabilizer subgroup of its action

annotations.note: Mathlib defines LieGroup and MulAction.stabilizer separately, but not the Klein-geometry "group model" combining them.
